_comment: pi-warden config. 'commands' = content rules (substring/regex) on bash commands, block-wins. 'paths' = directory containment across read/write/edit AND bash, longest-prefix wins; each path rule has on=read|write|any (default any) and action=block|allow|confirm. Each section default 'allow'=blacklist, 'block'=whitelist. /pi-warden governance unlock bypasses path rules for the session.

rules.reason: Recursive force-delete is reserved for the user. Note the path and ask them to run it.
The ~/.ssh directory is off-limits to the agent. Ask the user to provide what you need directly.
The vault rulebook (_core/) is readable, but writes need your confirmation — the agent must not rewrite the rules autonomously.
